records.ety: [OE. quarel, OF. quarrel, F. carreau, LL. quadrellus, from L. quadrus square. See Quadrate, and cf. Quadrel, Quarry an arrow, Carrel.]

defs: An arrow for a crossbow; -- so named because it commonly had a square head.
quotes: To shoot with arrows and quarrel.
Two arblasts, . . . with windlaces and quarrels.

defs: Any small square or quadrangular member
A square of glass, esp. when set diagonally.
A small opening in window tracery, of which the cusps, etc., make the form nearly square.
A square or lozenge-shaped paving tile.
A four-sided cutting tool or chisel having a diamond-shaped end.

records.ety: [OE. querele, OF. querele, F. querelle, fr. L. querela, querella, a complaint, fr. queri to complain. See Querulous.]

defs: A breach of concord, amity, or obligation; a falling out; a difference; a disagreement; an antagonism in opinion, feeling, or conduct; esp., an angry dispute, contest, or strife; a brawl; an altercation; as, he had a quarrel with his father about expenses.
quotes: I will bring a sword upon you that shall avenge the quarrel of my covenant.
On open seas their quarrels they debate.

defs: Ground of objection, dislike, difference, or hostility; cause of dispute or contest; occasion of altercation.
quotes: Herodias had a quarrel against him, and would have killed him.
No man hath any quarrel to me.
He thought he had a good quarrel to attack him.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Quarreled (?) or Quarrelled; p. pr. & vb. n. Quarreling or Quarrelling.]

defs: To violate concord or agreement; to have a difference; to fall out; to be or become antagonistic.
quotes: But some defect in her Did quarrel with the noblest grace she owed.

defs: To dispute angrily, or violently; to wrangle; to scold; to altercate; to contend; to fight.
quotes: Beasts called sociable quarrel in hunger and lust.

defs: To find fault; to cavil; as, to quarrel with one's lot.
quotes: I will not quarrel with a slight mistake.

defs: To compel by a quarrel; as, to quarrel a man out of his estate or rights.
One who quarrels or wrangles; one who is quarrelsome.
